items.claim: Chase Hooper needs to diversify his wrestling entries and shot selection to improve his grappling effectiveness.
items.quote: there needs to be more emphasis on wrestling. So, I'm I'm hoping, you know, Chase came up here and trained for a little bit. I wasn't here for those practices, but we've seen in in the Alex Hernandez fight, we saw in the Lance Gibson fight. I just feel like there needs to be more wrestling.

items.claim: Chase Hooper's guard is highly dangerous and he possesses very flexible hips.
items.quote: Chase is willing to roll the dice on himself and pull guard because his guard is that dangerous. He's got great leg locks. His hips are very, very, very flexible.

items.claim: Chase Hooper is physically large for his current weight class and has the potential to move up to welterweight.
items.quote: Something you guys don't know about Chase, he's [ __ ] massive. Like, the dude is [ __ ] huge. He is He's out of camp, he is as big as me. He's as tall as me. He weighs more than me. He's got big bones when you train with him. He's got big wrists. So, Chase is I think in due time he could become a welterweight.

items.claim: Chase Hooper is significantly larger than Mike Davis, making it easier for him to secure body triangles.
items.quote: And Chase is a lot bigger than Mike. It's a lot easier for him to get to that body triangle position.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez is entering the fight coming off a layoff, while Chase Hooper has been relatively active.
items.quote: for for Mitch he's coming in on a layoff and Chase has been relatively active and I think that that could play a big role in this fight.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez lacks the striking depth required to finish Chase Hooper.
items.quote: I just don't know if there's enough depth in the striking in Mitch's skill set to put Chase away.

items.claim: Christian Leroy Duncan's movement, footwork, and hand-eye coordination are derived from his background in basketball.
items.quote: In the NBA, you have to have really good footwork, especially moving side to side, good hand eye coordination. A lot of those things from basketball kind of apply to what Christian Leroy does inside the octagon.

items.claim: Christian Leroy Duncan excels at setting traps during his striking exchanges.
items.quote: the thing that stands out to me the most is I like that he sets trap.
